title: Default, Built-In, or Generic Account Usage on BES Hosts

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| lookup generic_accounts_blocklist.csv Account_Name OUTPUT blocked_account
| where isnotnull(blocked_account)
| stats count by Account_Name, ComputerName, bes_system
| sort - count
value: We flag logons using default or generic accounts that should be disabled or renamed per system hardening standards.
implementation: (1) Populate blocklist with Administrator, Guest, vendor defaults. (2) Tune for renamed admin accounts via SID lookup if available. (3) Page IR on hits. (4) Document approved break-glass in lookup.
visualization: Table (violations), Single value (event count), Bar chart (by account)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
